message:
Implement command execution timeout in SSH client

Problem:
Remote commands could hang indefinitely if workers become unresponsive,
blocking agent operations with no recovery path.

Solution:
Wrap both execute() and execute_streaming() methods with tokio::time::timeout
using the configurable command_timeout from SshOptions.

Implementation Details:
- execute(): Wraps the entire stdout/stderr reading and process wait in
  a single timeout future. On timeout, kills the child process and
  returns a clear error with the timeout duration.

- execute_streaming(): Wraps the line-by-line streaming loop and final
  wait in a timeout. On timeout, kills child and returns error. Already
  accumulated stdout/stderr are lost on timeout (acceptable trade-off
  for simplicity).

Timeout Behavior:
- Default timeout: Uses SshOptions.command_timeout (configurable)
- On timeout: Child process is killed via child.kill().await
- Error message: "Command timed out after {duration}"
- Logged at warn level with worker ID for debugging

Test Coverage:
- Add ssh_timeout_test.rs verifying SshOptions timeout configuration
- Cannot easily test real timeouts without mock SSH server, but
  structural correctness is verified through compilation and config tests

This addresses the fail-open philosophy: if a command times out,
the hook will fall back to local execution rather than blocking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
